Transform numeric variables to a log10 scale

Source: R/transform_log10.R
transform_log10.Rd

Adds log10-transformed copies of vars to dataf, prefixed with log10_. Original columns are preserved unchanged.

Usage

transform_log10(dataf, vars, overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)

Arguments

dataf

A data frame.

vars

Character vector of column names to transform. Must be numeric and strictly positive.

overwrite

If TRUE, replace existing log10_<var> columns. Default FALSE.

quiet

If TRUE, suppress warnings. Default FALSE.

Value

dataf with one new column per input variable, named log10_<var>.

Details

Errors when any value in vars is <= 0, since log10() is undefined there. Errors when a target column (log10_<var>) already exists, unless overwrite = TRUE.

Warns (does not error) if an input variable is itself already log10_-prefixed, since this typically indicates double-transformation.

Examples

df <- data.frame(x = c(10, 100, 1000), y = c(1, 2, 3))
transform_log10(df, vars = c("x", "y"))
#>      x y log10_x   log10_y
#> 1   10 1       1 0.0000000
#> 2  100 2       2 0.3010300
#> 3 1000 3       3 0.4771213
